pundit.name: Clint Dempsey
pundit.take: **Mustard Boy Zach's pickin' Saudi Arabia must be thinkin' their oil money gonna turn into goals, but all he got was 3 points and a world of hurt.** Meanwhile, Devin's Ghost Pepper is heatin' up with that Norway win over Iraq, sittin' pretty at 8 points — not bad, son, not bad. Rafe's The Noisemaker ain't even old enough to vote, yet this kid's atop the leaderboard like he's got cheat codes — Germany and Mexico out here puttin' in work for his 9 points. The Backpass Assassin, Gayden, tryin' to stay sneaky with 7 points, but it's more like he's passin' water than passin' through to the high ranks. And don't get me started on Bubba G Gunner. DR Congo in Tier 4? What, you got insider info from a fortune cookie? France saving your bacon with a 3-point haul, but you ain't lookin' crown-worthy. Zach, time to leave the mustard at the concession stand and put some ketchup on them scoreboards, brother.

pundit_takes.headline: Rafe the Noisemaker Maintains Top Spot Despite Drafting Non-Qualifiers
pundit_takes.subtitle: Beginners' luck reaches unprecedented levels in World Cup pool history.

pundit_takes.headline: Devin Ghost Pepper Poised to Narrowly Miss First Place Again
pundit_takes.subtitle: Spain and Norway combine to leave him perpetually in second.

pundit_takes.headline: Gunner Bubba G Relies on DR Congo for Unlikely Upset
pundit_takes.subtitle: Portugal matchup might test Gunner's optimism more than he planned.

pundit_takes.headline: Mustard Boy Aims for Redemption as Ghana Faces Panama
pundit_takes.subtitle: After a rough start, Zach needs a miracle to climb standings.

rome_analytics_quotes: Rafe is first with a shaky 16% win probability. Youthful chaos reigns supreme!
Rafe's Tier-3 teams outperforming Tier-1? Rookie madness or genius luck?
Gayden leads Rafe 3 points despite being outdone by his own son. Awkward.
Sim accuracy tanked the minute Rafe joined. Prediction game: officially broken.
